questions.text: What are the system boundaries of Auth and how do its internal packages (api, models, hooks, mailer, tokens, crypto, security) divide responsibilities?
What are the core data entities in Auth (User, Identity, Session, Factor, RefreshToken, OAuthClient, FlowState) and how do they relate?
What API endpoints does Auth expose and how are they organized (auth flows, admin, OAuth server, OIDC discovery, MFA)?
How does Auth handle JWT issuance, validation, and the hooks system for customizing auth behavior?
What external providers does Auth integrate with and how is the OAuth/OIDC flow implemented (including custom OAuth providers)?
What MFA mechanisms does Auth support (TOTP, WebAuthn/Passkeys, Phone) and how are they modeled?
What are the system boundaries of Realtime and how do its core modules (channels, tenants, PostgresCdc, extensions) interact?
How does Realtime implement its three features: Broadcast (ephemeral messages), Presence (shared state), and Postgres Changes (CDC)?
How does Realtime's multi-tenant architecture work — tenant isolation, connection management, replication, and the janitor/rebalancer processes?
How does Realtime authenticate and authorize WebSocket connections using JWT tokens and RLS policies (BroadcastPolicies, PresencePolicies)?
What are the system boundaries of Storage and how do its multi-protocol handlers (HTTP, TUS, S3, Iceberg) divide responsibilities?
What are the core data entities in Storage (Bucket, Object, S3MultipartUpload) and how does Postgres metadata relate to the actual object storage backend?
How does Storage handle authentication and authorization using JWTs and Postgres Row Level Security policies?
What are the system boundaries of the Studio monorepo and how do apps/studio, packages/pg-meta, and packages/api-types divide responsibilities?
What Postgres introspection capabilities does pg-meta provide (tables, columns, functions, policies, extensions, roles, views)?
What API type definitions does packages/api-types provide and how do they define the contract between Studio and the Supabase platform?
How does Studio authenticate users and manage project-level authorization for the admin dashboard?
What are the system boundaries of supabase-js and how do its sub-packages (auth-js, postgrest-js, realtime-js, storage-js, functions-js) compose into the unified client?
How does SupabaseClient initialize and wire together auth, database, realtime, storage, and functions sub-clients?
How does supabase-js handle cross-cutting concerns like auth token injection, session management, and fetch customization across all sub-clients?
What data flows from Auth to Storage and Realtime — how do JWTs issued by Auth propagate to authorize Storage uploads and Realtime WebSocket connections?
How does the Client SDK (supabase-js) abstract the contracts between Auth, Storage, Realtime, and PostgREST into a unified developer experience?
What shared concepts exist across services — how do 'tenant', 'project', 'JWT', 'RLS', and 'service_role' mean the same or different things in Auth vs Realtime vs Storage?
How does Studio (via pg-meta and api-types) interact with Auth, Storage, and Realtime services for project management?
